tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Tyrannosaurus Rex was one of the largest and most powerful land predators to ever exist on Earth.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It had massive jaws with powerful teeth designed for crushing bones and tearing flesh.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: T. Rex lived about 68 to 66 million years ago and became extinct during the mass extinction event.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Woolly Mammoth was a large prehistoric elephant-like animal that lived during the Ice Age.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It had thick fur, long curved tusks and a layer of fat to survive freezing climates.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Woolly Mammoths went extinct around 4,000 years ago.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Dodo was a flightless bird that lived on the island of Mauritius in the Indian Ocean.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It had a large body, small wings and could not fly.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Dodo became extinct in the late 1600s mainly due to human activity.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: The Saber-Toothed Tiger was a prehistoric predator known for its extremely long canine teeth.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It had powerful forelimbs and used its long fangs to hunt large animals.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Despite the name, it was not closely related to modern tigers.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Triceratops was a large herbivorous dinosaur known for its three horns and large frill.

tab2.title: Characteristics
tab2.content: It used its horns for defense against predators like Tyrannosaurus Rex.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Triceratops fossils are among the most commonly discovered dinosaur fossils.
